questions.question: In the SNT-TC-1A model, the certifying authority — the party whose written testimony makes a technician 'certified' — is:
options: The American Society for Nondestructive Testing
The employer
The customer whose contract invokes the document
The training school that delivered the coursework
questions.explanation: SNT-TC-1A describes an employer-based model: the employer establishes the written practice, administers the program, and issues certification — which is why that certification ends with employment.

questions.question: Which activity is within the proper scope of a trainee?
options: Performing calibrations alone per written instructions and recording results
Working alongside certified personnel under direct supervision, without independently performing, evaluating, or reporting tests
Interpreting indications when a Level II is busy
Signing reports that a Level II has verbally approved
questions.explanation: A trainee is in the program but not yet certified, so all work happens under direct supervision with no independent performance, evaluation, or reporting. Independent work per written instructions begins at Level I.

questions.question: Phased array ultrasonic testing stands in relation to ultrasonic testing as:
options: A technique within a method
A separate NDT method requiring its own written practice
A level of qualification
A referenced publication
questions.explanation: Ultrasonic testing is the method — a discipline built on one physical principle — while phased array is a technique within it; written practices may certify personnel to a method with technique limitations.

questions.question: Which qualification examination is normally closed book and deliberately contains no employer-specific content?
options: The specific examination
The practical examination
The general examination
The technical performance evaluation
questions.explanation: The general examination tests the principles of the method itself — physics, equipment classes, variables — independent of any one shop, so it is normally administered closed book.

questions.question: Under the recommended practice's typical model, maximum recertification intervals are:
options: One year for all levels
Three years for Levels I and II, five years for Level III
Five years for Levels I and II, ten years for Level III
Whatever each technician's customer requests
questions.explanation: The common SNT-TC-1A model recertifies Level I and II personnel at intervals not exceeding three years and Level III at intervals not exceeding five — with the written practice stating the binding values.

questions.question: Qualification examinations under an SNT-TC-1A written practice are administered and graded by:
options: Any certified Level II in the method
The NDT Level III or a designated representative identified in the written practice
The human resources department
An ASNT proctor in every case
questions.explanation: Examination administration and grading is the Level III's responsibility, though the written practice may name a designated representative to act on the Level III's behalf.

questions.question: A contract invokes ANSI/ASNT CP-189. The employer's written practice reduces the standard's minimum training hours for ET Level I, citing a documented in-house justification. This is:
options: Acceptable — written practices may always tailor requirements with justification
Acceptable — training hours are only recommendations in both documents
Not acceptable — the standard's minimums must be met or exceeded; written practices may tighten but not relax them
Not acceptable — only ASNT may write the employer's written practice under the standard
questions.explanation: Tailoring-with-justification belongs to SNT-TC-1A. CP-189 is a standard: its 'shall' requirements are floors, and an invoking contract makes any reduction a nonconformance.

questions.question: The grading rule stated by the standard (CP-189) for Level I and II qualification examinations is:
options: A composite of at least 70 percent with no examination below 60
A composite of at least 80 percent with no individual examination below 70
At least 90 percent on the practical examination only
A simple pass/fail decision left entirely to the examiner's judgment
questions.explanation: CP-189 states the rule plainly: composite grade of at least 80 percent and no individual examination grade below 70 percent — where the recommended practice leaves per-exam floors to the written practice.

questions.question: A candidate with a four-year mechanical engineering degree wants to sit the ASNT NDT Level III examinations. Under the program's published model, she needs at least:
options: No NDT experience — the degree fully substitutes
One year of NDT experience comparable to Level II work
Two years of NDT experience comparable to Level II work
Four years of NDT experience comparable to Level II work
questions.explanation: Education trades against experience but never to zero: a four-year engineering or science degree pairs with a minimum of one year of Level II-comparable NDT experience.

questions.question: An ASNT NDT Level III certificate holder adds a second method endorsement and later reaches the end of his validity period. Which statement is correct?
options: He repeats the Basic examination for every new endorsement and at every renewal
The certificate never expires once a second endorsement is added
Each endorsement required a new Method examination, and at the five-year mark he recertifies by reexamination or by documented professional credits
His employer can extend the certificate's validity under its written practice
questions.explanation: The Basic examination is passed once while certification remains in force; each endorsement adds a Method examination; validity is five years; and renewal is by reexamination or the program's documented professional-credits route — never by employer action.
